title: Candidate-aware multiclass decision interface

queries: graph llm candidate aware multiclass ranking decision interface autocomplete mrr
graph rag candidate scoring structured classification interface multiclass ranking

papers.title: Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
papers.claim: Graph-enhanced generation quality depends on aligning retrieval, evidence organization, and downstream decision interfaces.
papers.relevance: Supports redesigning the multiclass decision interface rather than treating label tokens as a shallow afterthought.

papers.title: G-Retriever: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Graph Question Answering
papers.claim: Graph evidence must be consumed through an interface matched to the downstream decision problem, not merely compressed into a generic prompt.
papers.relevance: Supports a candidate-aware scoring interface for autocomplete-style ranking tasks.

papers.title: Can we Soft Prompt LLMs for Graph Learning Tasks?
papers.claim: Prompt-side graph/task interface design can dominate gains once encoder-side modifications saturate.
papers.relevance: Supports moving from a verbalizer tweak to a more structural multiclass decision interface.

evidence.why_now: EXP081 showed that autocomplete label-interface unification nudges item-incoterms in the right direction but is still too weak. EXP087 then showed that baseline rerun variance is large enough to confound near-boundary interpretation, and EXP090 finally showed that the EXP078 split local/global packaging family does not survive replay. With loss, sampling, basis gating, and packaging-nearby branches either saturated or replay-failed, the next justified step is a broader candidate-aware multiclass decision interface.

hypothesis: The salt-side bottleneck is not only in graph evidence quality but in how multiclass candidates are represented and scored. A broader candidate-aware multiclass interface should keep both the class-id view and the semantic raw-label view of each candidate, then let the prompt-conditioned sample representation fuse those two candidate views at scoring time. This should preserve ranking discrimination better than raw-label verbalizers alone or class-id-only scorer unification alone.
notes: Implemented and launched on 2026-05-07 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: failed.

Proposed minimum mechanism: for autocomplete multiclass tasks only, build per-candidate representations from both class-id text and raw-label text, then score candidates with a prompt-conditioned fusion of the two views.
No new loss family, no sampling tweak, and no packaging-nearby rerun should be mixed into this first implementation.
First stable orchestrator status check shows all three windows up; user-churn and user-ltv completed DB load, while item-incoterms remained in early salt startup.
Outcome: user-churn improved strongly, but user-ltv regressed and item-incoterms stayed in the same regressive MRR band as recent packaging/control runs.
This should be treated as the next architecture-review output, not as an excuse to resume blind lightweight scanning.
